label: Opposing Data Centers in Great Lakes Region

comments.text: Saline Township, a small Michigan town with a 3K population, is going up against OpenAI and Oracle's Stargate. The experts warn the data centers' undisclosed water and electricity usage could strain resources, especially near Lake Michigan. #ProudBlue #DataCenters #Michigan

I want data centers to not use all the water in the Great Lakes. I want rent control and no new apartments, but my friends should all be able to move here.

"The Great Lakes are an important, binational resource that more than 40 million people depend on for their drinking water and supports a US$6 trillion regional economy. Data centers compete with these existing uses and may deplete local groundwater aquifers."

Why are data centers booming in the Great Lakes?
The Great Lakes hold 20% of the world’s surface freshwater.
But this abundance is deceptive: between 40%-75% of Great Lakes residents depend on groundwater for drinking water, and groundwater shortages are already occurring in every Great Lakes state.

With access to unlimited ground water and the freshwater of the Great Lakes, this is not surprising.
